description: MCPS-070 (MCPS-EPIC-P6.6C). The checked-in, machine-checkable map from each MCP-S security property (P6.6A + P6.6B) to the Bazel test target AND the named test function that PROVES it. Docs reference this manifest ('see the manifest') instead of stale literal counts. Guarded by //mcps-conformance:security_traceability_guard_test (MCPS-070): every entry's bazel_target must be declared by a real BUILD.bazel rule and every test_fn must appear in the referenced test source on disk. A renamed or removed target/fn makes that guard FAIL. Each entry's source is read from DISK at test time via runfiles (the same $(rlocationpath) scheme as the conformance drift_guard); nothing is trusted as written.

entries.property: request object signature verified; tampered request body rejected over the wire
entries.notes: demo_negative A1. Mutating a signed param byte breaks the request-object signature; the proxy rejects before any forward. Mirrors mcps-core vector v2_tampered_argument.json.

entries.property: tampered JSON-RPC id rejected over the wire
entries.notes: demo_negative A2. The signed id binding is broken by editing the id; rejected. Mirrors mcps-core vector tampered_id.json.

entries.property: core conformance vectors: tampered body/id + replay/expiry/audience/envelope (object == stdio == http parity)
entries.notes: The committed mcps-core vectors (tampered_id.json, v2_tampered_argument.json, replay_request.json, expired_request.json, wrong_audience_request.json, missing_envelope_request.json) are replayed in-process. Cross-transport parity is in http_harness_test / stdio_harness_test. The vector corpus itself is drift-guarded by conformance_manifest.json + drift_guard_test.

entries.property: freshness + replay rejected on a fresh connection (replay across connection)
entries.notes: demo_negative A3. A previously-accepted signed request replayed on a NEW mTLS connection is rejected by the proxy's nonce/freshness cache — replay protection spans connections, not just one socket.

entries.property: freshness: expired request rejected over the wire
entries.notes: demo_negative A4. A request whose signed expiry is in the past is rejected (freshness window enforced).

entries.property: replay detected across a proxy RESTART via the shared durable nonce cache
entries.notes: P6.6B C2. A request accepted by one proxy process is replayed after the proxy is killed and a fresh process starts against the SAME durable cache; the replay is still rejected. Durable, not in-memory-only.

entries.property: response signing + request_hash binding, independently verified by the client
entries.notes: demo_e2e_persistent. Each signed response is verified against the request hash the HostSession STORED at sign time, and independently re-verified via mcps_demo::independently_verify_response against the server signer's public key (a path independent of the client's own session state).

entries.property: wrong response hash rejected by the client over the wire
entries.notes: demo_negative A9. A response bound to the wrong request hash fails client-side verification. Mirrors mcps-core vector v4b_signed_wrong_hash_response.json.

entries.property: bad response signature rejected by the client over the wire
entries.notes: demo_negative A10. A response with a garbage/forged signature fails client-side verification. Mirrors mcps-core vector v4_wrong_hash_garbage_sig_response.json.

entries.property: HostSession sign produces a request the verifier accepts (round trip)
entries.notes: HostSession sign/verify foundation: the session signs with the injected clock + RNG and the verifier accepts.

entries.property: HostSession verifies a response against the STORED request hash, not a caller-supplied value
entries.notes: HostSession sign/verify: response correlation uses the hash the session itself stored at sign time; a caller cannot inject the binding hash.

entries.property: HostSession rejects a response bound to the wrong request hash
entries.notes: HostSession sign/verify: a response whose bound hash does not match the stored request hash is rejected.

entries.property: mTLS client/server happy path: trusted server + client round-trip succeeds
entries.notes: mTLS transport happy path: trusted server cert + trusted client cert complete a mutual-auth round trip.

entries.property: server-cert verification: untrusted server cert REJECTED by the client
entries.notes: Server-auth case 1 of 4. A server cert from a CA the client does not trust is rejected during the handshake.

entries.property: server-cert verification: trusted server cert ACCEPTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 2 of 4 (trusted-accepted). A server cert chaining to the client's trusted server CA is accepted (same fn as the happy path).

entries.property: server-cert verification: wrong server identity REJECTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 3 of 4. A trusted-CA server cert presenting the WRONG identity (SAN) is rejected — chain validity alone is not sufficient.

entries.property: server-cert verification: expired server cert REJECTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 4 of 4. A server cert past its validity window is rejected.

entries.property: server-cert verification: empty server CA fails closed (no implicit trust)
entries.notes: Defense-in-depth for the four server-auth cases: an empty server trust anchor set does not silently accept any server — it fails closed.

entries.property: transport-binding EXACT: identity (mTLS client cert URI SAN) must equal the request signer
entries.notes: demo_transport T3. A valid mTLS client whose cert SAN identity does NOT equal the request signer is rejected: the transport identity is bound exactly to the signer.

entries.property: valid mTLS does NOT rescue an invalid request signature (transport != authn of the object)
entries.notes: Every demo_negative A* case runs over a fully VALID mTLS connection, yet the tampered/forged request object is still rejected — a valid transport channel never rescues a broken object signature. A1 is the canonical witness.

entries.property: transport: untrusted server cert refused by the client in the demo end-to-end path
entries.notes: demo_transport T5. End-to-end echo of the server-auth property: the demo host client refuses an untrusted proxy server cert.

entries.property: Phase-5 allow/deny over a persistent session; admin tool DENIED before dispatch
entries.notes: Per-scope grants over one persistent inner session; the admin tool (reset_items) has NO grant and is scope-denied. inner_request_forwarded (emitted inside dispatch_and_sign) is the deny-before-dispatch probe — a denied call shows zero forwards.

entries.property: admin tool denied before dispatch, end-to-end (denied call never forwarded to the persistent inner)
entries.notes: demo_e2e_persistent. The admin reset_items call (no grant) is denied with mcps.authorization_scope_denied; the inner's own received-log (an INDEPENDENT signal) shows it was never forwarded.

entries.property: persistent inner reused: one spawn, multiple forwards (single process serves N calls)
entries.notes: P6.6B. N authorized requests are served over ONE long-lived inner process (one spawn, many forwards).

entries.property: deny-not-reached via the inner received-log: a pre-dispatch denial yields zero forwards while the inner stays alive
entries.notes: The anti-gaming oracle for 'denied never reached': the inner's forward count stays at zero for a denied call and the inner process is NOT torn down.

entries.property: demo server received-log lists ONLY calls the inner actually served (independent oracle)
entries.notes: Validates the received-log oracle itself: it records only executed calls, so 'denied never reached' assertions built on it are sound.

entries.property: per-request strip/inject: the proxy is the SOLE writer of .verified, fresh on every request of a persistent session
entries.notes: MCPS-064. A forged caller-owned .verified block (inside the signed params) is stripped on EVERY request across one long-lived session and the sidecar-owned context for request K is injected fresh — the proxy is the sole writer (ADR-MCPS-008).

entries.property: caller-supplied .verified is stripped and replaced even when it is part of the signed payload (in-process)
entries.notes: In-process unit witness of the strip/replace property: a signed-in .verified block is still stripped before forwarding.

entries.property: env-keysource fail-closed: production binary refuses --key-source env without --allow-env-keysource (no listener bound)
entries.notes: P6.6B C1. The production CLI exits non-zero and binds NO listener when env key material is offered without the explicit opt-in flag (parse-time gate).

entries.property: env-keysource compile-time gate (MCPS-076): production binary fails closed on --key-source env EVEN WITH --allow-env-keysource (EnvKeySource not compiled)
entries.notes: MCPS-076 (audit gap G-3): EnvKeySource is gated behind the non-default dev_env_key_source feature, so the production binary refuses env key material at construction even with the runtime opt-in; the diagnostic names the required feature rebuild.

entries.property: env key source loads + signs (MCPS-076): dev-feature build reads the seed and signs correctly WITHOUT mutating the process environment (issue #25)
entries.notes: MCPS-076 (audit gap G-3) + issue #25: the dev/CI-only EnvKeySource (feature dev_env_key_source) loads all material and signs. The read is now a PURE read — it does NOT remove the seed env var (the prior std::env::remove_var was unsound under threads); child-process secret isolation is the inner-launch env policy's job (no env inheritance by default), not a global mutation here. This is the positive control for the env-keysource posture, in a feature-gated unit test rather than the production binary.

entries.property: key-source errors never leak secret material
entries.notes: Defense-in-depth for the env-keysource path: error messages from the key source never echo the secret seed.

entries.property: authorization binding: Core binds on_behalf_of and authorization_hash into the signed object, surfaced to the AuthorizationProfile (Core binds; profile interprets)
entries.notes: §A Delegation / authorization binding (ADR-MCPS-013). The signed request carries on_behalf_of + authorization_hash inside the signing scope; the verifier accepts and the verified object surfaces verified.on_behalf_of == ON_BEHALF_OF and verified.authorization_hash == AUTH_HASH for the configured AuthorizationProfile to interpret. Core BINDS; it never validates artifact contents, provides RBAC, or emits a mismatch (coverage Partial). Same fn as the round-trip foundation entry; this entry names the authorization-binding property §A maps.

entries.property: trust-cache / revocation-tier: a bounded trust cache admits an active key and denies an unknown key (fails closed)
entries.notes: §A Revocation / trust propagation → §B Axis 2 (ADR-MCPS-021). A BoundedTrustCache wrapping the ADR-022 authorized-key-set resolver admits an active node key and fails closed on an unknown key. The bounded-window tier surfaces its own honest guarantee (never zero-window). §B Axis 2's live/push tiers are additionally exercised by the proxy_unit_test revocation_tier/live_trust/push_trust vectors re-derived by drift_guard.

entries.property: keyset-admission: per-node keys accepted, unknown/revoked rejected, shared-remote-signer mode (Axis 3 blast radius)
entries.notes: §A Key custody / blast radius → §B Axis 3 (ADR-MCPS-022) AND §A Signer identity. Per-node Ed25519 keys are each an explicit member of the authorized key set (node-a and node-b accepted); the sibling cases per_node_keyset_unknown_key_rejected / per_node_keyset_revoked_key_rejected fail closed, and shared_remote_signer_only_shared_key_accepted covers the single-pinned-identity mode. One compromised node revokes only that node's key — never a smaller blast radius than per-node.

entries.property: audit-evidence vocabulary drift (ADR-MCPS-035): every emitted rejection reason is a member of the frozen McpsError::wire_code() taxonomy and the success-event set is exactly the two-item allowlist
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-036 gate item (c) / issue #151. The audit vocabulary (mcps-core/src/audit.rs) mints no rejection reason outside the frozen wire_code() taxonomy (error.rs is the sole authority), read from disk via runfiles. Companion assertions in the same target keep the success allowlist exact (success_event_set_is_exactly_the_two_item_allowlist) and forbid any authorization_hash_mismatch notion (no_authorization_hash_mismatch_audit_reason).

entries.property: forbidden-claim drift (ADR-MCPS-036): no forbidden over-claim wording appears as an asserted live claim in the proposal-facing docs
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-036 gate item (d) / issue #155. The forbidden-wording list (co-located with §A's Forbidden-claim column) does not appear as a live claim in security-boundary / v0.5-claim-matrix / threat-coverage / composability / proposal-scope, read from disk via runfiles. A phrase inside a Forbidden-claim table cell or a negated sentence is legitimately naming the non-goal and is allowed.

entries.property: method-transparency (ADR-MCPS-030): the Core verdict is independent of the JSON-RPC method — identical verdict across tools/list, tools/call, resources/list, prompts/list, and a fabricated x/nonexistent/custom, for an accepted AND a rejected envelope
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-034 behavioral equivalence proof (extends ADR-MCPS-030). Each method is re-signed (method is in signing scope, ADR-MCPS-026), so the proof is verdict PARITY across honestly-signed envelopes — a strictly stronger claim than mere acceptance of known methods. The rejected family (rejected_verdict_is_identical_across_all_methods) holds one defect (expired window) constant while only the method varies, proving the method neither rescues nor worsens the verdict; accepted_and_rejected_verdicts_are_distinct keeps the parity non-vacuous.

entries.property: method-transparency is durable (ADR-MCPS-030): no non-test mcps-core/src code references a concrete MCP method-name literal (tools/list, tools/call, resources/list, resources/read, prompts/list, prompts/get, sampling/createMessage, completion/complete)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-034 static drift guard (extends ADR-MCPS-030). Scans every *.rs in mcps-core/src (delivered via runfiles, dir re-read so new files are covered), excluding the trailing #[cfg(test)] region of each file, and FAILS if any banned method-name literal appears in production Core. The bare "method" field is deliberately NOT banned (bare_method_word_is_not_banned) — Core must still sign/canonicalize the full object; guard_inputs_are_non_empty_and_exclusion_is_load_bearing proves the test-region exclusion is exercised so the guard cannot silently false-pass.

counts.comment: Recorded count. The guard re-derives this from the entries array length and FAILS on mismatch.
